source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In a railway line between the cities $X$ and $Y$, the trains can meet, traveling in opposite directions, only in one of its stretches, in which the line is double. The trains take 180 minutes to go from $X$ to $Y$ and 60 minutes to go from $Y$ to $X$, at constant speeds. On this line, a train can start from $X$ at the same instant that a train starts from $Y$, without them colliding during the trip. Which of the following figures represents the line?
<image1>
extraction_notes: 选项字段中只有选项字母，具体选项内容应在图片中。
题目明确要求从图形中判断，明显依赖图像。

image_dependency: The question asks 'Which of the following figures represents the line?', so selecting an answer depends on inspecting figure options.
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image asset.
multi_step: The text gives asymmetric travel times (180 minutes from X to Y, 60 minutes from Y to X) and constant speeds, which implies quantitative reasoning about relative motion.
The condition that trains start simultaneously from opposite ends and avoid collision requires combining timing/position reasoning with the location of the single double-track stretch.
The final step is to map the derived constraint to one of several figure options.
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice and answer_text is a single label 'B'.
The problem constraints are explicit in the text (travel times, constant speeds, simultaneous departure, only one double-track stretch).
registry_passed=true and the image asset exists, so the designated option can be checked against the provided figure set.

risk_flags: choices are label-only in text (A-E); actual option content is only available via the image
verification of the chosen option depends on visual access to the figure

decision_reasons: Question text, answer, and required image asset are all present, so the core semantics are intact.
Asset registry passed with no recorded issues.
The sample has high image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability scores, indicating strong candidate value.
Although option content is visually grounded, the necessary image exists, so this does not block recovery or verification.

asset_records.normalized_text_snapshot: On a railway line from X to Y, trains can pass each other only on a single double-track stretch. A train takes 180 minutes to go from X to Y, while a train takes 60 minutes to go from Y to X, both at constant speeds. If one train leaves X at the same time another leaves Y and they do not collide, where along the line must the double-track stretch be located?
